summary: Prioritize a VWAP-hold continuation entry filter and tighten invalidation logic for weak midday volume.
state_assessment: Evidence quality is moderate-high; setup appears testable under current execution constraints.

hypotheses.title: VWAP hold continuation after earnings gap-up
hypotheses.thesis: Gap-up leaders that reclaim and hold VWAP in the first 30 minutes are more likely to continue through day-one high.
hypotheses.problem_target: False continuation entries with weak early-session structure
hypotheses.mechanism: Early institutional participation is proxied by sustained VWAP hold plus above-baseline opening volume.
evidence_basis: Losers often lose VWAP before noon

proposed_rule_changes.change: Require 5-minute close above VWAP for 3 consecutive bars before break of day-one high.
Invalidate if price closes below VWAP with relative_volume_5m < 1.2 before 12:00.

hypotheses.expected_impact: Lower false positives and improved trade quality at cost of fewer entries.
key_risks: May reduce trade count in lower-volatility earnings windows
VWAP hold may lag in fast trend acceleration
kill_criteria: If 30-trade sample shows expectancy <= 0 after costs
If median adverse excursion worsens by >20% versus baseline

minimum_viable_experiment.goal: Test whether VWAP hold precondition improves net expectancy.
minimum_viable_experiment.setup: Backtest 2019-01 to latest; compare baseline vs VWAP-hold variant.

hypotheses.rationale: Strong fit with observed winner traits and feasible with existing features.
assumptions: Relative volume remains predictive in current regime
dependencies: Reliable intraday VWAP and 5m volume data

selected_next_actions: Run A/B backtest baseline vs VWAP-hold variant
Review slippage impact around macro event windows

logging_payload.objective: Improve consistency of an earnings gap-up continuation setup
selected_next_actions: Run A/B backtest baseline vs VWAP-hold variant
Review slippage impact around macro event windows
